title: Maximum Profit in Job Scheduling
prompt:
You are given `n` jobs where every job is scheduled to be done from `startTime[i]` to `endTime[i]`, obtaining a profit of `profit[i]`.

You're given the `startTime`, `endTime`, and `profit` arrays. Return the maximum profit you can take such that there are no two jobs in the subset with an overlapping time range.

If you choose a job that ends at time `t`, you may start another job that starts at time `t`.

examples.output:
120
Explanation: Choose jobs 0 and 3 (time 1-3 with profit 50, time 3-6 with profit 70) for total 120.

6
Explanation: All jobs overlap so pick the most profitable single job.

export function solve(startTime: number[], endTime: number[], profit: number[]): number {

notesForAI.coreIdea: Sort jobs by end time. Use DP where dp[i] = max profit considering first i jobs. For each job, binary search for the latest non-overlapping previous job. dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], profit[i] + dp[last_non_overlapping]).
commonBugs: Forgetting to sort by end time before applying DP
Off-by-one in binary search for the latest non-overlapping job
Using start time instead of end time for the overlap check (a job ending at t can chain with one starting at t)
